snippet: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
snippet: The US has a 15-point plan to end the war, with Iran offering 10 proposals of its own. Can they be reconciled?

title: UK's NATO show of force ends with docked destroyer in Mediterranean after 'technical' issue
snippet: The U.K. docked its sole naval warship in the Mediterranean due to technical issues amid growing criticism of PM Starmer's response to the Iran war.

title: Bowen: Ceasefire means respite for civilians, but it might not last long
snippet: Whether or not a lasting peace deal can be reached, the war and its consequences are reshaping the Middle East, writes international editor Jeremy Bowen.

title: Evangelical leaders rally for Trump and Israel as Operation Epic Fury reshapes the region
snippet: Evangelical leaders say support for Israel has remained strong during Operation Epic Fury, the joint U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran, as a new ceasefire takes hold.

title: North Korea fires missiles toward sea after ridiculing South's hopes for better ties
snippet: North Korea fired multiple ballistic missiles after criticizing South Korea’s push for improved relations, raising tensions in the region.

title: Iran's UN ambassador takes swipe at Trump in final hours before Strait of Hormuz deadline
snippet: Iran's UN ambassador is calling one of President Donald Trump's posts on Truth Social on Tuesday “deeply irresponsible" and “profoundly alarming."

title: Australia’s most decorated living soldier charged amid fierce debate over war crimes allegations
snippet: Australia's most decorated living soldier Ben Roberts-Smith arrested and charged with allegedly murdering five unarmed Afghans in Afghanistan.

title: China aiding Iran missile program amid US-Israeli strikes, reports say
snippet: China is reportedly sending missile fuel materials to Iran to help rebuild its missile program amid continuing U.S.-Israeli strikes aimed against the Tehran regime.

title: Russia, China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz, hours before Trump deadline
snippet: Russia and China vetoed a U.N. resolution to reopen the Strait of Hormuz hours before President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to stop threatening the vital waterway.

title: Victory over the kingdom of evil
snippet: The richest country – the poorest people

title: How TALMA turns English education into social mobility and a Zionist mission
snippet: At the Jerusalem Post Miami Conference, TALMA CEO Ido Mahatzri showed how teachers, language and values reshape Israel’s periphery.

title: 'Over 90% of Iranians hate the regime,' INSS’s Beni Sabti reveals from leaked Iran survey
snippet: “It was a secret poll which said that 92% of the Iranian people hate their regime,” Sabti said, according to a transcript provided to The Jerusalem Post.

title: Alshareef: Israel, Jews, should help Iranians seek liberty to ‘repay debt to Cyrus’
snippet: Speaking at the Jerusalem Post Miami Summit on Wednesday, Alshareef called on Israel and the Jewish people to back Iranians seeking freedom, as Netanyahu voiced solidarity with the protesters.

title: Several dead after Israeli joint security forces raid in West Bank

June 17, 2024 - Israel-Gaza news
snippet: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has disbanded the country's war cabinet, an Israeli official says, just over a week after opposition leader Benny Gantz withdrew from the body.

title: June 13, 2024 Israel-Hamas war
snippet: The US-backed Gaza ceasefire proposal doesn't meet Hamas' demands for an end to the war, a senior official has told CNN. Osama Hamdan also said that "no one has an idea" how many of the hostages held in the enclave are alive.

title: June 12, 2024 Israel-Hamas war
snippet: Israel launched strikes on Al-Mawasi in southern Gaza early Thursday, bombarding an area it had previously designated as a "safe zone" by land, sea and air, according to the official Palestinian news agency WAFA.

title: June 10, 2024 Israel-Hamas war
snippet: The UN Security Council adopted a resolution proposed by the United States for a permanent ceasefire and release of the hostages in Gaza.

title: June 10, 2024 - Israel-Hamas war
snippet: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has called on Benny Gantz to change his mind after the key war carbinet member resigned from government over the handling of the war in Gaza.

title: June 9, 2024 Israel-Hamas war
snippet: Key Israeli war cabinet member Benny Gantz quit Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government Sunday. Meanwhile, the death toll from Israel's hostage rescue in central Gaza keeps rising. Follow for live updates.

title: June 8, 2024 - Israel-Gaza news
snippet: The Israeli military says it rescued four hostages from central Gaza on Saturday. Palestinians report scenes of horror on the ground, and Gaza hospital officials say more than 200 people were killed.

title: June 6, 2024 Israel-Hamas war
snippet: Dozens of people were killed in an Israeli airstrike on a United Nations-run school in central Gaza, authorities said, in an attack that CNN analysis found was conducted with a US-made weapon.

title: June 5, 2024 Israel-Hamas war
snippet: Tensions are ramping up at the Israel-Lebanon border, with Hezbollah claiming responsibility for a Wednesday evening attack in northern Israel that left multiple people injured.

title: June 1, 2024 - Israel-Gaza news
snippet: President Joe Biden has laid out a three-phase proposal to end the conflict in Gaza, as global condemnation of Israel's offensive in Rafah grows.

title: May 30, 2024 Israel-Hamas war
snippet: The war in Gaza will likely continue until at least the end of the year, an Israeli official warned.
